title: Saudi Arabia vs Uruguay prediction and betting tips 15 June 2026 | Dailysports experts
original_text:
Saudi Arabia vs Uruguay prediction and betting tips 15 June 2026
Bielsa faces serious personnel issues. Prediction for the World Cup match between Saudi Arabia and Uruguay
Photo: x.com/goal.com/ Author unknownn
In the opening round of the 2026 World Cup group stage, Saudi Arabia will square off against Uruguay. The clash is set for the night of Tuesday, June 16, with the opening whistle sounding at 00:00 Central European Time. I'm offering a bet on the goal total in this encounter.
Read also: World Cup match prediction Belgium vs Egypt: ambitions put to the test from the very first round, 15.06.2026
H2H and statistical facts
Saudi Arabia has kept a clean sheet in two consecutive matches.
The Saudis have won just one of their last eight matches in regular time.
Uruguay is winless in four straight games.
The South American side did not play a single friendly immediately before the World Cup began.
The teams have met three times: one draw and one win apiece.
At World Cups, Uruguay and Saudi Arabia have faced off only once: Luis Suárez secured victory in the 2018 group stage.
A pivotal match for the Saudis
Saudi Arabia has once again qualified for the World Cup and will be aiming to replicate their 1994 feat, when the team advanced to the knockout stage. In recent years, the "Green Falcons" have been regulars at the world’s top tournament, and at the last World Cup, they made headlines with a sensational win over Argentina.
Preparation for the tournament was mixed. At the end of March, the Saudis lost to Egypt (0-4) and Serbia (1-2), but they showed marked improvement just before the World Cup. The team defeated Puerto Rico 3-0, then played solid football in a goalless draw with Senegal.
A notable feature of this Saudi squad is the recent coaching change, as they have played only three matches under the guidance of Giorgos Donis. The team is also undergoing a generational shift and is eager to prove they can compete with more high-profile opponents.
The opening match is hugely significant for Saudi Arabia. With upcoming games against group favorites Spain and an ambitious Cape Verde side, even a draw with Uruguay could dramatically boost their chances of fighting for a place in the knockout rounds.
Read also: A historic chance for the 'Persian Leopards': prediction for Iran vs New Zealand - 16.06.2026
Uruguay out to prove its favorite status
Uruguay enters the tournament as one of the favorites to progress from the group. Marcelo Bielsa’s team came through South American qualifying in style and brings to the World Cup a squad that is both experienced and youthful, with Federico Valverde and Ronald Araújo playing key roles.
Unlike many other World Cup participants, Uruguay played a minimal number of friendlies this year. In March, they drew 1-1 with England and then failed to score against Algeria (0-0). Ahead of the tournament, the coach opted to skip friendlies altogether.
The coaching staff has faced personnel problems on the eve of the tournament. Due to injuries, several key players—including Ronald Araújo and Giorgian De Arrascaeta—are doubtful or sidelined. Nevertheless, Bielsa has stated that the team will not abandon its aggressive style and intends to take the game to their opponents.
Additional complications have arisen during the World Cup itself. Uruguay has faced logistical issues and flight delays en route to the USA, but the squad is confident this won’t affect their readiness for the opener. A win over Saudi Arabia would be a crucial step toward the knockout stage—even before their decisive clash with Spain.
Probable lineups
Saudi Arabia: Al-Owais, Abdulhamid, Al-Tambakti, Al-Amri, Al-Harbi, Kanno, Al-Juwair, Al-Khaibari, Madash, Al-Dawsari, Al-Buraikan.
Uruguay: Muslera, Viña, Olivera, Cáceres, Varela, Ugarte, Bentancur, Araújo, Valverde, Viñas, Núñez.
Where's the smart money?
Uruguay comes into the match as the favorite thanks to a higher level of talent and vast experience on the world stage. However, Saudi Arabia has proven time and again that they're capable of springing surprises at the World Cup, so Marcelo Bielsa’s men shouldn’t expect an easy ride. My pick is under 2.5 total goals.
Prediction on game Total under 2.5
Odds: 1.78
